intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as established specimens. Only P3 (Pothos) is identifiable via the white rabbit anchor, though it appears as a singular, struggling sprout.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant material detected in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant material detected in the black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Single sprout visible with white rabbit anchor.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant material detected.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of growth.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of growth.
plant_audit.P3: Single green sprout emerging from soil near the rabbit anchor. Appears to be a Pothos cutting or seedling.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of growth.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dark, damp, and appears to contain perlite or crushed eggshell fragments.
biome_observations.debris: White fragments (likely eggshell) present in P3 pot.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-2: No significant change; soil remains dark and empty.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Lighting shift to blue-spectrum dominance; no growth detected.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Return to standard LED spectrum; P3 sprout remains static in position and size.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of extreme dormancy or failure. P1, P2, and P4 are effectively non-existent. P3 is the only viable specimen, currently in a fragile, early-stage growth phase.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of white particulate matter (eggshells) in P3; absence of all other registered species.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is severely under-populated. The registry suggests a diverse collection, but the visual evidence confirms a near-total loss of specimens, with only a single Pothos sprout surviving in the P3 container. The soil appears consistently moist, yet there is no evidence of secondary growth or weeds, suggesting a sterile or highly controlled substrate.
